summary: Updated the admin load-test dashboard to use backend API targets by default, add per-virtual-user request identity, omit admin cookies, and allow target URLs to be edited from the UI.
changed: Default load-test targets now point to /api/public/works and /api/public/blogs endpoints.
Work read and Study read URLs are editable in the dashboard.
Load-test fetches omit credentials to avoid admin-cookie bias.
Vitest and Playwright coverage was updated.
notChanged: No distributed load generator was added.
No k6 or JMeter integration was added.
No backend endpoint behavior was changed.
Unrelated dirty files were left untouched.
validations: npx skills find "load testing docker backend k6 playwright"
BACKEND_PUBLISH_PORT=18080 NGINX_HTTPS_PORT=3002 docker compose -f docker-compose.dev.yml up -d --build frontend nginx

PLAYWRIGHT_EXTERNAL_SERVER=1 PLAYWRIGHT_BASE_URL=http://127.0.0.1:3000 PLAYWRIGHT_E2E_PROFILE=exhaustive npx playwright test tests/admin-load-test-dashboard.spec.ts --project=chromium-authenticated --workers=1 --grep "admin can run"
risks: Browser-driven load generation is still limited by browser connection behavior.
Absolute cross-origin target URLs may require CORS support.
Production-grade distributed capacity testing still needs a server-side or external load generator.
recommendation: Use this dashboard for backend API read-load checks and Docker stats observation; add k6/server-side load generation for production capacity testing.
